paper.title: The rocket effect mechanism in neutron stars in supernova remnants

review.summary: This is a theoretical/modelling paper that applies the electromagnetic rocket effect model to a sample of 13 pulsars associated with SNRs. It contains 6 internal LaTeX tables: a summary of kick mechanisms (review), a sample population table of 13 pulsars with observed parameters, a braking index catalogue of 16 pulsars, derived kick velocity results from two models, calculated magnetic axis displacement values, and a table of characteristic quantities for two case-study pulsars (J0030+0451 and J0538+2817). No external data resources (URLs) are provided — the data availability statement indicates data are available upon request. The paper uses public catalogues (ATNF, SIMBAD, SNRcat) as data sources but does not provide direct URLs to machine-readable versions.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summary of the three main kick mechanisms for neutron stars (hydrodynamical, neutrino-driven, rocket effect), providing context for the paper's focus on the electromagnetic rocket effect.

source_refs.caption: The main kick mechanisms for neutron stars.
source_refs.label: kicks

columns.name: Mechanism
columns.meaning: Name of the kick mechanism (Hydrodynamical, Neutrino Driven, Rocket effect)

columns.name: Velocity
columns.meaning: Typical kick velocity imparted to the neutron star, in km/s

columns.name: Alignment of kick-spin
columns.meaning: Whether spin-kick alignment is expected for this mechanism

columns.name: Natal/Post-natal
columns.meaning: Whether the kick occurs at birth (natal) or after birth (post-natal)

columns.name: Timescale
columns.meaning: Duration over which the kick mechanism operates

columns.name: Requirements
columns.meaning: Physical conditions required for the mechanism to be effective

internal_tables.evidence: Section 1 Introduction, paragraph discussing kick mechanisms. The table summarizes hydrodynamical, neutrino-driven, and rocket effect kicks.
internal_tables.comments: This is a qualitative review/summary table, not a data catalogue.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary sample definition — presents the 13 neutron stars associated with supernova remnants that are analysed in this work, with their observed physical parameters sourced from the ATNF pulsar catalogue and SNR catalogues.

source_refs.caption: The sample population of 13 neutron stars associated with supernova remnants.
source_refs.label: table:1

columns.name: PSR NAME
columns.meaning: Pulsar J-name identifier

columns.name: SNR
columns.meaning: Associated supernova remnant name

columns.name: P
columns.meaning: Current spin period of the pulsar

columns.name: Pdot
columns.meaning: Period derivative (spin-down rate)

columns.name: B
columns.meaning: Surface magnetic field strength inferred from spin-down

columns.name: V_t
columns.meaning: Transverse space velocity of the pulsar

internal_tables.evidence: Section 2.3 Sample of pulsars. Data sourced from ATNF catalogue (Manchester et al. 2005), SNRcat (Ferrand & Safi-Harb 2012), and SIMBAD (Wenger et al. 2000).
internal_tables.comments: This is the core sample table. Parameters P, Pdot, B, V_t are taken from the ATNF catalogue. SNR associations from SNRcat and SIMBAD.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Catalogue of measured braking indices n for 16 pulsars (some overlap with the main sample), compiled from literature, used to assess whether the rocket effect can explain deviations from the canonical value n=3.

source_refs.caption: Braking index of the 16 pulsars of our catalogue along with the corresponding references.
source_refs.label: 3.1t

columns.name: Pulsar Name
columns.meaning: Pulsar J-name identifier

columns.name: n
columns.meaning: Measured braking index (dimensionless parameter describing spin-down law: ωdot ∝ ω^n)

columns.name: Article
columns.meaning: Reference for the braking index measurement

internal_tables.evidence: Section 2.5 Braking indices. Values compiled from various literature sources cited in the table.
internal_tables.comments: 16 pulsars with measured braking indices, used to compare against theoretical predictions of the rocket effect model.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Primary results table — presents the kick velocities computed from two models (Harrison-Tademaru HT and the authors' time-dependent alternative AL) for each of the 13 pulsars, showing initial spin period and SNR age used in calculations.

source_refs.caption: The results of the kick velocity for our population of 13 pulsars. The velocities are in km/s, and they have been measured by both models.

columns.name: Pulsar Name
columns.meaning: Pulsar J-name identifier

columns.name: P_in
columns.meaning: Initial spin period, calculated from Equation (tau_c) using SNR age instead of characteristic age

columns.name: tau_snr
columns.meaning: Age of the associated supernova remnant

columns.name: V_kick^HT
columns.meaning: Kick velocity computed using the Harrison & Tademaru (1975) model (Eq. 1 of paper)

columns.name: V_kick^AL
columns.meaning: Kick velocity computed using the authors' alternative time-dependent model (Eq. 10 of paper)

internal_tables.evidence: Section 3.1 Kick Velocities. Calculations assume R=12 km, M=1.44 M_sun, s=10 km. P_in derived following Igoshev et al. (2022) methodology.
internal_tables.comments: Key result table. For pulsars where characteristic age > SNR age (leading to negative square root), P_in is assumed to be 1-2 ms.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Shows the required magnetic axis displacement s (distance from star centre) needed for the rocket effect to produce a kick equal to the observed transverse velocity, assuming P_in = 1 ms for all pulsars.

source_refs.caption: In this table is shown the value of the displacement s of the axis of the magnetic field and the center of the star, so that the star has a kick velocity equal to the transverse component of the current space velocity V_t. For these calculations we take into consideration an initial spin period of 1 ms for all pulsars.
source_refs.label: 3.5t

columns.name: Pulsar Name
columns.meaning: Pulsar J-name identifier

columns.name: V_t
columns.meaning: Observed transverse space velocity of the pulsar

columns.name: s
columns.meaning: Required distance of the magnetic axis from the star's centre so that the rocket effect kick equals V_t

internal_tables.evidence: Section 3.1, end of subsection. Equation (10) is solved for s with P_in = 1 ms fixed.
internal_tables.comments: All s values fall within the allowable range (0-10 km given R=12 km). Largest s=9.77 km for J1809-1917.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compiles the characteristic observed quantities for the two case-study pulsars J0538+2817 and J0030+0451, including period, period derivative, characteristic age, parallax, proper motion, transverse velocity, distance, magnetic field, mass, and radius — used as inputs for individual MCMC analyses.

source_refs.caption: Characteristic quantities of the pulsars J0538+2817 and J0030+0451 that are used in the MCMC analysis.
source_refs.label: 4.2t

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Physical parameter name

columns.name: J0538+2817
columns.meaning: Value of the parameter for pulsar J0538+2817

columns.name: J0030+0451
columns.meaning: Value of the parameter for pulsar J0030+0451

internal_tables.evidence: Section 3.3 Case studies. Parameters from literature: period and period derivative from timing solutions; parallax and proper motion from astrometry; V_t derived from proper motion and distance; B from spin-down formula.
internal_tables.comments: Rows include: Period (ms), Period Derivative (s/s), τ_c (yrs), π (mas), μ (mas/yr), V_t (km/s), D (kpc), B (G), Mass (M_sun), R (km). Some values only available for J0538+2817.
